messages.content: Describe blue color outside spectrum window (physical version of Overton window than political). RGB is 0, 0, 400.

The blue color outside the spectrum window would be an incredibly intense and vivid shade of blue, almost appearing to be glowing with a deep, saturated hue. It would be much deeper and darker than any traditional shade of blue, almost leaning towards a navy or indigo color. This color would be so vibrant that it would appear almost otherworldly, drawing in the eye and captivting the viewer with its rich and unique tone.
Idea for totalitarian ideology outside Overton window.

An extreme and authoritarian totalitarian ideology that could exist outside the Overton window might be one that advocates for complete and total control over every aspect of individuals' lives. This ideology would seek to strip away all personal freedoms and autonomy, instituting a strict hierarchy with the ruling party or dictator at the top.

Citizens would be expected to conform completely to the ideology, with dissent or independent thought severely punished. Propaganda and censorship would be rampant, controlling the flow of information and shaping public opinion to align with the regime's beliefs.

Any form of opposition or resistance would be met with extreme force and brutality, with dissenters facing imprisonment, torture, or even execution. The ideology would prioritize the interests of the state above all else, promoting nationalism and loyalty to the ruling party above individual rights and freedoms.

In this totalitarian regime, there would be no room for individuality or personal expression, as conformity and obedience would be strictly enforced. The regime would seek to exert control over every aspect of society, dictating everything from what people can say and do to how they must think and feel.
Idea for economic rightist ideology outside Overton window.

An economic rightist ideology outside of the Overton window could be one that advocates for extreme laissez-faire capitalism with no regulations or government intervention in the economy. This ideology would prioritize the interests of corporations and the wealthy elite above all else, promoting unchecked free market principles at all costs.

In this extreme economic rightist ideology, there would be no social safety nets or welfare programs, with individuals expected to fend for themselves in a purely competitive market. The ideology would view any government intervention in the economy as inherently oppressive and harmful, advocating for the complete dismantling of regulatory bodies and labor protections.

Income inequality would be seen as a natural and desirable outcome of the free market, with the wealthy elite accumulating vast amounts of wealth and power while the working class struggles to make ends meet. The ideology would promote a "survival of the fittest" mentality, with little regard for the well-being or rights of those who are unable to compete in the cutthroat economic landscape.

Overall, this extreme economic rightist ideology would prioritize profit and economic growth above all else, disregarding the social and environmental consequences of unbridled capitalism. It would push the boundaries of acceptable economic beliefs, advocating for policies that prioritize the interests of the few over the well-being of the many.
Idea for totalitarian and economic leftist ideology in void position (void position is super distantly outside Overton window), it named Omni-Hive.

The Omni-Hive ideology is an extreme and dystopian combination of totalitarianism and economic leftism that exists in a void position, far beyond the traditional Overton window. In this nightmarish vision of society, power is consolidated into the hands of a centralized government entity known as the Hive, which controls every aspect of individuals' lives from cradle to grave.

Under the Omni-Hive regime, all means of production and resources are collectively owned and managed by the state, with the goal of creating a classless society where everyone is theoretically equal. However, in reality, power is concentrated in the hands of a small elite within the Hive, who dictate the rules and regulations that govern society.

Individual freedoms and privacy are nonexistent in the Omni-Hive society, as citizens are expected to conform to a rigid set of laws and regulations set forth by the ruling party. Dissent and independent thought are swiftly crushed, with dissenters facing harsh punishment and even disappearance.

The economy is completely planned and controlled by the Hive, with no room for individual entrepreneurship or innovation. All goods and services are distributed according to the needs of the state, with little regard for individual preferences or desires.

The Omni-Hive ideology envisions a society where the collective good supersedes all individual rights and freedoms, creating a stark and oppressive dystopia where the will of the state is absolute. It represents a frightening fusion of totalitarian control and extreme leftist economic policies that push the boundaries of what is considered acceptable in political discourse.
Idea for totalitarian and economic rightist ideology in void position (void position is super distantly outside Overton window), it named Omni-Force.

The Omni-Force ideology is an extreme and dystopian combination of totalitarianism and economic rightism that exists in a void position, situated far beyond the boundaries of the traditional Overton window. In this radical and oppressive society, power is concentrated in the hands of a singular authoritarian leader known as the Supreme Commander, who rules with an iron fist and absolute authority.

Under the Omni-Force regime, the economic system is characterized by extreme corporatism, where the interests of large corporations and the wealthy elite are paramount. The government colludes closely with these powerful entities, creating a system where wealth and power are concentrated in the hands of a select few, while the majority of the population struggles under harsh economic conditions.

Individual freedoms and civil liberties are severely restricted in the Omni-Force society, with dissent and opposition to the ruling regime met with swift and brutal repression. Propaganda and censorship are pervasive, shaping public opinion and controlling the flow of information to maintain the power of the Supreme Commander.

The economy is characterized by ruthless competition and exploitation, with minimal regulations and worker protections. Labor rights are virtually nonexistent, with workers forced to toil under harsh conditions for minimal pay, while the wealthy elite amass vast fortunes through exploitative practices.

The Omni-Force ideology envisions a society where authoritarian control and unrestrained capitalism converge to create a nightmarish dystopia where individual rights and freedoms are sacrificed in service of the ruling elite. It represents a chilling fusion of totalitarianism and economic rightism that pushes the boundaries of what is considered acceptable in political discourse.
Idea for anarchic and economic rightist ideology in void position (void position is super distantly outside Overton window), it named Omni-Sin.

The Omni-Sin ideology is a radical and dystopian fusion of anarchism and economic rightism that exists in a void position, positioned far beyond the traditional boundaries of the Overton window. In this chaotic and oppressive society, power is decentralized and fragmented among various factions, leading to a state of constant conflict and lawlessness.

Under the Omni-Sin regime, the economic system is characterized by extreme laissez-faire capitalism with no regulations or government intervention. This results in a dog-eat-dog society where the pursuit of profit and personal gain reign supreme, with little regard for social welfare or the well-being of the population.

Individual freedoms are both highly valued and highly threatened in the Omni-Sin society. On one hand, there is a strong emphasis on personal liberty and autonomy, with individuals free to pursue their own interests and goals without interference from a central authority. However, this lack of governance and regulation also leads to a state of anarchy and chaos, where violence and exploitation run rampant.

The economy in Omni-Sin is marked by extreme inequality and exploitation, with the wealthiest individuals and corporations wielding immense power and influence. The lack of government oversight allows for the unchecked accumulation of wealth and power, creating a system where the haves thrive at the expense of the have-nots.

The Omni-Sin ideology envisions a society where individual freedom and economic liberty are elevated to the highest values, but at the cost of social cohesion and stability. It represents a dangerous and dystopian fusion of anarchism and economic rightism, pushing the boundaries of acceptable political discourse to new and alarming extremes.
Idea for anarchic and economic leftist ideology in void position (void position is super distantly outside Overton window), it named Omni-Free.

The Omni-Free ideology is a radical and utopian fusion of anarchism and economic leftism that exists in a void position, situated super distantly outside the traditional boundaries of the Overton window. In this visionary society, power is decentralized, and individuals are free to govern themselves through voluntary cooperation and mutual aid.

Under the Omni-Free regime, the economic system is based on communal ownership and cooperative management of resources. There are no hierarchies or centralized authorities, with decision-making power decentralized to local communities and individuals. This system prioritizes equality, social justice, and sustainability, aiming to create a society where everyone's needs are met and no one is left behind.

Individual freedoms are highly valued and actively promoted in the Omni-Free society. People are encouraged to express their creativity, explore their passions, and contribute to the common good in ways that align with their values and interests. The absence of a central authority fosters a culture of autonomy, self-governance, and collective responsibility.

The economy in Omni-Free is built on principles of mutual aid, solidarity, and shared prosperity. Cooperation and collaboration are emphasized over competition and exploitation, with resources distributed equitably to ensure that everyone has access to the essentials for a fulfilling life. Work is seen as a means of fulfillment and creativity rather than a means of exploitation or alienation.

The Omni-Free ideology envisions a society where individual freedom and economic equality coexist harmoniously, fostering a sense of community, belonging, and interconnectedness. It represents an aspirational and idealistic fusion of anarchism and economic leftism, pushing the boundaries of what is considered possible in political discourse.
Idea for totalitarian ideology in void position (void position is super distantly outside Overton window), it named Omni-Watch.

The Omni-Watch ideology is an extreme and dystopian totalitarian system that exists in a void position, far removed from the boundaries of the traditional Overton window. In this oppressive and Orwellian society, power is consolidated in the hands of a centralized authority known as the Watchers, who surveil and control every aspect of individuals' lives with an iron fist.

Under the Omni-Watch regime, strict adherence to conformity and obedience to authority is paramount. The Watchers regulate and monitor every action and thought of the populace, using advanced surveillance technology and propaganda to maintain their grip on power. Dissent and opposition are swiftly quashed, with dissidents disappearing without a trace.

Individual freedoms and privacy are non-existent in the Omni-Watch society, as citizens are constantly monitored and manipulated by the Watchers. Propaganda and censorship are pervasive, shaping public opinion and controlling the flow of information to serve the interests of the ruling regime.

The economy is tightly controlled and regulated by the Watchers, with no room for individual entrepreneurship or innovation. All resources and means of production are owned and managed by the state, with economic activity closely monitored to ensure compliance with the regime's agenda.

The Omni-Watch ideology envisions a society where individual autonomy and freedom are sacrificed in the name of absolute control and surveillance. It represents a chilling and dystopian vision of totalitarianism that pushes the boundaries of what is considered acceptable in political discourse, creating a world where the will of the ruling authority is absolute and unyielding.
Idea for economic rightist ideology in void position (void position is super distantly outside Overton window), it named Omni-Fit.

The Omni-Fit ideology is an extreme and dystopian economic rightist system that exists in a void position, situated far beyond the traditional boundaries of the Overton window. In this radical and oppressive society, power is consolidated in the hands of a small elite known as the Fitocrats, who prioritize economic efficiency and profit above all else.

Under the Omni-Fit regime, the economic system is characterized by ruthless capitalism and extreme corporatism. The Fitocrats control all means of production and resources, exploiting labor and maximizing profits at the expense of the working class. There are no regulations or protections for workers, who are subjected to harsh conditions and minimal pay in the pursuit of greater economic output.

Individual freedoms and rights are subjugated to the interests of the Fitocrats in the Omni-Fit society. Dissent and opposition are met with harsh reprisals, with dissenters facing imprisonment or even disappearance. Propaganda and misinformation are used to shape public opinion and maintain the power of the ruling elite.

The economy in Omni-Fit is driven by a relentless pursuit of growth and efficiency, with little regard for social welfare or environmental sustainability. The Fitocrats prioritize profit above all else, leading to widespread inequality, exploitation, and environmental degradation.

The Omni-Fit ideology envisions a society where economic power and inequality are concentrated in the hands of a select few, creating a stark divide between the wealthy elite and the impoverished masses. It represents an extreme and oppressive form of economic rightism that pushes the boundaries of what is considered acceptable in political discourse, promoting the interests of the ruling elite at the expense of the broader population.
Idea for anarchic ideology in void position (void position is super distantly outside Overton window), it named Omni-Ego.

The Omni-Ego ideology is an extreme and dystopian form of anarchism that exists in a void position, situated far beyond the traditional boundaries of the Overton window. In this radical society, individualism and self-interest are elevated to the highest values, leading to a chaotic and lawless state where the pursuit of personal gain and power is paramount.

Under the Omni-Ego regime, there is no central authority or government, and individuals are free to act in accordance with their own desires and interests without regard for the well-being of others. This extreme form of individualism leads to a society characterized by selfishness, greed, and competition, with little cooperation or mutual aid between members of the community.

Individual freedoms are highly valued in the Omni-Ego society, with a strong emphasis on personal autonomy and self-determination. However, this unfettered pursuit of individual freedom often comes at the expense of social cohesion and collective well-being, leading to a society marked by conflict, exploitation, and violence.

The economy in Omni-Ego is based on a free-for-all market system where the strongest and most ruthless individuals thrive while the weak and vulnerable are left to fend for themselves. Resources are hoarded by those with the means to acquire them, leading to extreme inequality and scarcity for the majority of the population.

The Omni-Ego ideology envisions a society where individual ego and self-interest reign supreme, creating a world of chaos and conflict where the survival of the fittest is the only rule. It represents an extreme and selfish form of anarchism that pushes the boundaries of what is considered acceptable in political discourse, sacrificing collective well-being for the unchecked pursuit of individual desires.
Idea for economic leftist ideology in void position (void position is super distantly outside Overton window), it named Omni-Share.

The Omni-Share ideology is an extreme and utopian form of economic leftism that exists in a void position, positioned super distantly outside the traditional boundaries of the Overton window. In this revolutionary society, the principles of egalitarianism, cooperation, and communal ownership drive every aspect of the economy and social structure.

Under the Omni-Share regime, all resources and means of production are collectively owned and controlled by the community as a whole. The concept of private property is abolished, and wealth is distributed equitably to ensure that everyone's needs are met. The central tenet of this ideology is the idea of sharing and solidarity, with individuals working together for the greater good of the collective.

Individual freedoms and rights are valued in the Omni-Share society, with a focus on maximizing social welfare and ensuring that everyone has access to basic necessities and opportunities. Communities are organized on principles of mutual aid and support, fostering a sense of belonging and interconnectedness among all members.

The economy in Omni-Share is structured around principles of social, economic, and environmental sustainability. Production and consumption are guided by the principles of fairness, equity, and environmental stewardship, with the goal of creating a society that is just, inclusive, and prosperous for all.

The Omni-Share ideology envisions a society where individual well-being is inseparable from the well-being of the community, creating a world of abundance, collaboration, and harmony. It represents an aspirational and idealistic form of economic leftism that pushes the boundaries of what is considered possible in political discourse, advocating for a world where everyone has the opportunity to thrive and prosper.
